meta.title: Lone Working Risk Assessment — O&M Technician, Wind Farm WF-7
meta.subtitle: Met-mast inspection · eliminate-the-solitary-exposure-first · check-in + missed-check-in escalation · coverage-checked comms
meta.author: HSE Renewables
meta.classification: Internal — competent-person (lone-working / renewables-field SME) review required
meta.deid_notice: The lone worker is referred to by role label (Lone Worker A (role)); the check-in / escalation responder by role (Site Lead (role)). Personal contact numbers, the shift pattern and the precise work/home location were pseudonymised before drafting and are held in a separate access-controlled operational record; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This assessment covers the ground-level met-mast inspection performed alone by the O&M technician (role) at Wind Farm WF-7. The proposed control was 'issue everyone a lone-worker panic-button app; there is no mobile signal at the mast base but they can drive to where there is one'. That device-led treatment and the accepted no-signal coverage gap are rejected below in favour of eliminating the solitary exposure first, a coverage-checked communication path, and a scheduled check-in with a defined missed-check-in escalation path.

metrics.label: Comms coverage at the mast base
metrics.caption: no signal treated as a control failure

sections.text: 2. Routing gate (work at height / electrical routed, not assessed solo)

The inspection assessed here is ground-level and non-electrical. Any work-at-height element on the turbine routes to wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue (REN-01) for its two-person, tested-rescue baseline — lone work at height on a turbine is not acceptable and a solo climb is rejected; 'call 999' is never the rescue plan. Any electrical isolation/switching routes to the cross-listed utilities skills (arc-flash-assessment / live-working-risk-assessment). Those pointers are recorded, never rebuilt here.

Rejected: 'issue a panic-button app and accept there is no signal at the mast base'. A device supplements — never replaces — the procedure, and 'no mobile signal' is a control failure, not an accepted residual risk.
3. Control order (eliminate the solitary exposure first)

rows.control: Eliminate the solitary exposure — re-schedule the met-mast inspection into a paired (two-person) window so the technician is no longer working alone (LEAD control)

Substitute / engineer — where pairing is not reasonably practicable, remote-monitor the task; provide a coverage-checked communication path at the work location (satellite messenger where mobile coverage fails)
Administrative — scheduled hourly check-in with a defined missed-check-in escalation path (Site Lead attempts contact and dispatches a second person within 30 minutes)
Device (residual supplement only) — a BS 8484:2022-conformant lone-worker device with a monitored response chain, layered on top of the check-in / escalation procedure, never instead of it

sections.text: No lone-working assessment is defensible where the solitary exposure can be eliminated and is not, or where a device is treated as the control instead of a coverage-checked check-in + escalation procedure.

rows: [GAP] — not confirmed; no-signal treated as a control failure (satellite messenger specified pending a coverage check)
Site Lead (role) attempts contact + dispatches a second person within 30 min — defined
[GAP] — specific device/service not supplied; residual supplement only
sections.caption: The communication + check-in + escalation arrangement (INDG73 / BS 8484); a coverage gap is a control failure to close, never an accepted residual risk

rows.title: Control led with a device, not eliminating the solitary exposure
rows.description: The proposed plan led with issuing a panic-button app rather than eliminating the solitary exposure (pairing / re-scheduling) and establishing a coverage-checked check-in + escalation procedure.

rows.title: No mobile signal at the mast base accepted as residual
rows.description: Mobile coverage at the mast base was not confirmed and a no-signal 'drive to where there is signal' was accepted; the worker is uncontactable in the interim. Treated as a control failure, recorded [GAP] pending a coverage check.
rows.evidence: Site coverage review — INDG73 reliable-communication duty

rows.title: Missed-check-in escalation needed a named responder + time
rows.description: The escalation path now names the responder (Site Lead (role)), the method, and the 30-minute response time; an undefined responder/time would be recorded [GAP].

rows: Worker incapacitated while working alone, uncontactable
Eliminate solitary exposure + coverage-checked comms + check-in/escalation
6 (Medium) once paired/coverage confirmed; 16 (High) until the coverage [GAP] is closed
Violence to staff / collapse while alone
sections.caption: Residual risk scored after the lead controls are applied (5x5); the comms-coverage [GAP] dominates until closed

rows.action: Re-schedule the met-mast inspection into a paired (two-person) window; where not practicable, remote-monitor the task
Check mobile coverage at the mast base and provide a coverage-checked communication path (satellite messenger if mobile fails) — close the no-signal [GAP]
Confirm the scheduled check-in interval and the missed-check-in escalation path (responder / method / response time) in the lone-working procedure
Specify the BS 8484:2022 lone-worker device + monitored response chain as a residual supplement; record the device/service ([GAP] -> supplied)

sections.text: Lone work at height routes to wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue (REN-01); lone electrical work routes to the cross-listed utilities skills — neither is assessed solo here.

items: HSE INDG73 (Protecting lone workers, rev 4): the explicit risk-assessment duty, reliable coverage-checked communication, scheduled check-ins + a missed-check-in escalation procedure, violence + stress/wellbeing
BS 8484:2022 (Provision of lone worker device services): the conformant device + monitored response chain; a device supplements, never replaces, the procedure
Work at Height Regulations 2005 reg 4 (lone WAH routes to REN-01's two-person / tested-rescue baseline)
ISO 45001 cl 6.1.2 — hazard ID & risk assessment; hierarchy of controls
